9434.title: Posttraumatic Stress Disorder and Obstructive Sleep Apnea in Twins.
kf: Vietnam Era Twin Registry to test PTSD-OSA association controlling for shared genetics and early environment
PTSD severity associated with higher OSA prevalence within twin pairs
Causal interpretation: PTSD increases OSA risk independent of familial confounders
9434.why: Strongest available evidence for secondary service connection of OSA to PTSD
9434.pop: Male twins from Vietnam Era Twin Registry

9434.title: Studying the Prevalence of PTSD in Veterans, Combatants and Freed Soldiers of Iran-Iraq War.
kf: Pooled prevalence of PTSD in combatants ~38 percent.
Prevalence in freed POWs substantially higher.
Time since exposure did not reduce prevalence — chronic course.
9434.why: Supports chronicity of combat PTSD; relevant for service connection and TDIU.
9434.pop: Veterans, combatants, freed POWs of Iran-Iraq War across multiple studies

9434.title: The effect of obstructive sleep apnea severity on PTSD symptoms during the course of esketamine treatment: a retrospective clinical study.
kf: More severe OSA attenuated PTSD response to esketamine
OSA severity inversely correlated with PTSD improvement
Bidirectional clinical interaction
9434.why: OSA worsens PTSD treatment outcomes, reinforcing aggravation/secondary connection arguments
9434.pop: Veterans receiving esketamine for PTSD at VA San Diego

9434.title: Psychological and pharmacological interventions for PTSD and comorbid mental health problems following complex traumatic events.
kf: Multicomponent psychological interventions (TF-CBT, EMDR) reduced PTSD symptoms with moderate-to-large effect sizes.
Combined treatments addressing comorbid depression and anxiety produced additional benefit in complex PTSD.
Pharmacotherapy alone produced smaller effects than psychotherapy.
9434.why: Supports that combat veterans with comorbid depression and anxiety warrant multicomponent treatment.
9434.pop: Adults with PTSD following complex traumatic events across 116 RCTs

9434.title: Combat-Related PTSD and Comorbid Major Depression in U.S. Veterans: The Role of Deployment Cycle Adversity and Social Support.
kf: Comorbid major depression occurred in over half of U.S. veterans with combat-related PTSD.
Higher deployment cycle adversity predicted greater comorbid depression severity.
Lower post-deployment social support was independently associated with PTSD-MDD comorbidity.
9434.why: Strong evidence supporting secondary service connection of MDD to combat PTSD.
9434.pop: U.S. OEF/OIF/OND veterans with combat-related PTSD

9434.title: Epigenome-wide meta-analysis of PTSD across 10 military and civilian cohorts identifies methylation changes in AHRR.
kf: DNA methylation changes in AHRR gene associated with PTSD across 10 cohorts.
Biological signature of PTSD persists across trauma type and cohort.
Epigenetic markers may serve as future biomarkers.
9434.why: Provides biological evidence supporting legitimacy of the diagnosis in disability adjudication.
9434.pop: Pooled cohort ~1,896 PTSD cases and ~4,498 controls from military and civilian populations

9434.title: The Efficacy of Cognitive Processing Therapy for PTSD Related to Military Sexual Trauma in Veterans: A Review.
kf: CPT demonstrates significant efficacy for PTSD secondary to MST.
Veterans receiving CPT show clinically meaningful symptom reduction.
Gender-sensitive delivery improves engagement and outcomes.
9434.why: Supports MST-related PTSD as service-connected with established treatment.
9434.pop: Veterans (male and female) with PTSD secondary to MST

9434.title: Benzodiazepines for PTSD: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
kf: Benzodiazepines are ineffective for PTSD treatment and may worsen outcomes.
Benzodiazepine use was associated with increased aggression, depression, and substance misuse in veterans with PTSD.
Evidence supports avoiding benzodiazepines in PTSD; trauma-focused psychotherapy and SSRIs remain first-line.
9434.why: Supports argument that veterans on benzodiazepines may have inadequately treated PTSD.
9434.pop: 18 clinical trials and 12 observational studies including veteran samples

9434.title: Meta-analysis of the efficacy of treatments for posttraumatic stress disorder.
kf: Psychotherapy (notably trauma-focused CBT and EMDR) produced large effect sizes for PTSD symptom reduction.
SSRIs and SNRIs produced moderate effects; risperidone, topiramate, and venlafaxine had supportive evidence.
Benzodiazepines lacked evidence of benefit and are not recommended for PTSD.
9434.why: Foundational evidence supporting service-connected PTSD treatment efficacy.
9434.pop: 137 RCTs of psychotherapy and pharmacotherapy for PTSD, including VA and military samples

9434.title: Review: managing PTSD in combat veterans with comorbid traumatic brain injury.
9434.journal: Journal of rehabilitation research and development
kf: PTSD-TBI comorbidity highly prevalent in OEF/OIF veterans; complicates treatment.
Symptom overlap between PTSD and post-concussive syndrome can confound diagnosis.
Integrated multidisciplinary care improves outcomes.
9434.why: Supports secondary service connection of PTSD to documented TBI events.
9434.pop: OEF/OIF combat veterans with comorbid PTSD and mild TBI

9434.title: Prevalence estimates of combat-related post-traumatic stress disorder: critical review.
9434.journal: The Australian and New Zealand journal of psychiatry
kf: Lifetime PTSD prevalence among Vietnam veterans 10-30 percent depending on methodology.
Current PTSD prevalence in OEF/OIF veterans 4-17 percent.
Higher combat exposure consistently correlated with elevated PTSD risk.
9434.why: Authoritative prevalence data supporting combat-related PTSD as service-connected.
9434.pop: Combat veterans from Vietnam, Gulf War, OEF/OIF, and allied cohorts
